title: mastigloscleria reflexa
intro: Mastigloscleria reflexa is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the cattail or bulrush family. It is a type of sedge that is native to tropical Africa, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and reduce swelling. In herbal preparations, Mastigloscleria reflexa is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory problems and gastrointestinal issues.
